title: Haig P. Manoogian

infobox.value: ([/ˈheɪɡ məˈnuːdʒiən/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); May 23, 1916 – May 26, 1980) was an Armenian-American professor of film at [New York University](/wiki/New_York_University "New York University") who served as the main influence for many filmmakers such as [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese"), who was a student of his at New York University. Martin Scorsese called Manoogian teachings “The most precious gift I have ever received.”

[""With Love and Resolution": An Appreciation"](https://www.neh.gov/humanities/2013/marchapril/feature/love-and-resolution-appreciation). *neh.gov*. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20190701161645/https://www.neh.gov/humanities/2013/marchapril/feature/love-and-resolution-appreciation) from the original on July 1, 2019. Retrieved January 9, 2020.

outgoing_links.text: Who's That Knocking at My Door

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American academic (1916–1980)

**Haig Manoogian** ([/ˈheɪɡ məˈnuːdʒiən/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); May 23, 1916 – May 26, 1980) was an Armenian-American professor of film at [New York University](/wiki/New_York_University "New York University") who served as the main influence for many filmmakers such as [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese"), who was a student of his at New York University. Martin Scorsese called Manoogian teachings “The most precious gift I have ever received.”

## Martin Scorsese

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Haig_P._Manoogian&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Martin Scorsese")]

Manoogian co-produced Scorsese's first feature film *[Who's That Knocking at My Door](/wiki/Who%27s_That_Knocking_at_My_Door "Who's That Knocking at My Door")*. Martin Scorsese's classic film *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")* is dedicated to him. Scorsese has named Manoogian as one of his largest inspirations.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
